title: Complete Guide to Rice Export Process from Pakistan
excerpt: Learn the step-by-step process of exporting rice from Pakistan, including documentation, shipping, and quality requirements for international markets.
author: Royal Rice Mills
content:
Introduction

Pakistan is one of the world's largest rice exporters, supplying premium basmati and non-basmati varieties to over 100 countries. The rice export process involves multiple steps, from procurement to final shipment.

Step 1: Procurement & Paddy Selection

The process begins with procuring paddy from farmers across Punjab and Sindh provinces. Our team carefully selects paddy based on grain length, moisture content, and variety specifications.

Step 2: Milling & Processing

Selected paddy undergoes de-husking, whitening, polishing, and color sorting at our modern milling facility equipped with Japanese and German machinery.

Step 3: Quality Testing

Every batch is tested in our in-house laboratory for moisture percentage, broken ratio, chalky grains, color consistency, and foreign matter. We maintain ISO and HACCP standards throughout.

Step 4: Packaging

Rice is packaged in food-grade PP woven bags or custom-branded retail packs ranging from 1kg to 50kg. Vacuum packing is available on request for extended shelf life.

Step 5: Export Documentation

We prepare all necessary documents including Commercial Invoice, Packing List, Certificate of Origin, Phytosanitary Certificate, Bill of Lading, and SGS/quality certificates as required.

Step 6: Shipment

Containers are loaded at Karachi Port or Port Qasim and shipped worldwide. We offer both FOB and CIF terms with reliable shipping partners.

Conclusion

Royal Rice Mills handles the complete export chain from farm to port, ensuring quality at every step. Contact us for competitive pricing and dedicated export support.

title: Basmati vs IRRI Rice: Complete Comparison Guide
excerpt: Understand the key differences between Basmati and IRRI rice varieties in terms of grain length, aroma, pricing, and best use cases for importers.
author: Royal Rice Mills
content:
Overview

When sourcing rice from Pakistan, buyers often choose between two main categories: Basmati rice and IRRI (non-basmati) rice. Both have distinct characteristics suited for different markets.

Grain Length

Basmati rice features extra-long grains (7.5-8.6mm) that elongate up to 2.5 times during cooking. IRRI varieties have medium-long grains (6.0-6.8mm) with moderate elongation.

Aroma & Flavor

Basmati rice is famous for its distinctive natural aroma, making it the preferred choice for pilaf, biryani, and premium dishes. IRRI rice has mild to neutral aroma, suitable for everyday meals.

Price Comparison

Basmati rice commands a premium price due to its superior quality and limited growing regions. IRRI rice is more affordable, making it popular for bulk purchases by food businesses and retail chains.

Best Markets

Basmati rice is highly demanded in the Middle East, Europe, and North America. IRRI rice finds strong demand in Africa, Southeast Asia, and China.

Which Should You Import?

Your choice depends on your target market and pricing strategy. Many successful importers stock both varieties to serve diverse customer segments.

title: How to Store Rice for Long Term: Expert Tips
excerpt: Expert tips on storing rice properly to maintain quality, prevent pest infestation, and extend shelf life for wholesalers and distributors.
author: Royal Rice Mills
content:
Why Proper Storage Matters

Proper rice storage is crucial for maintaining grain quality, preventing moisture damage, and avoiding pest infestation. Whether you're a wholesaler, retailer, or exporter, following correct storage practices ensures your investment stays protected.

Temperature Control

Rice should be stored at temperatures between 15-20°C. High temperatures accelerate aging and can lead to discoloration. Avoid direct sunlight exposure.

Moisture Management

Ideal storage moisture is 12-14%. Higher moisture levels invite mold growth and insect activity. Use moisture meters to regularly check stored rice.

Proper Ventilation

Warehouses must have adequate ventilation to prevent condensation. Stack bags on pallets, keeping them 50cm away from walls for air circulation.

Pest Prevention

Regular fumigation and inspection prevents weevil and moth infestations. Use food-grade insecticides and maintain strict hygiene in storage facilities.

Packaging Best Practices

Sealed PP woven bags with polyethylene lining offer the best protection. Vacuum-sealed packaging extends shelf life to 18-24 months for retail products.

title: Private Label Rice Packaging: Complete Guide for Importers
excerpt: Everything you need to know about starting your own rice brand with private label packaging from Pakistan's leading rice mills.
author: Royal Rice Mills
content:
What is Private Label Rice?

Private label (or own brand) rice packaging allows importers and retailers to sell premium Pakistani rice under their own brand name. This strategy builds brand loyalty and increases profit margins.

Benefits of Private Labeling

Key advantages include higher profit margins, brand recognition, customer loyalty, and control over pricing strategy in your market.

Our Private Label Process

We offer end-to-end private label service: from design consultation and bag printing to filling, quality sealing, and export documentation under your brand.

Minimum Order Requirements

Typical MOQ for private label orders is one full container (20-25 MT). Smaller trial orders can be discussed on a case-by-case basis.

How to Get Started

Contact our export team with your requirements including rice variety, packaging design, bag sizes, and target quantity. We'll provide samples and competitive quotations.
